title: Multi-layer porous block copolymer films
abstract: The present disclosure relates to methods of making multi-layered graded multiblock copolymer films; multi-layered graded multiblock copolymer films made by the disclosed methods; uses of the disclosed multi-layered graded multiblock copolymer films; and devices comprising the disclosed multi-layered graded multiblock copolymer films. An exemplary disclosed multi-layered graded multiblock copolymer film has at least three identifiable layers comprising a first porous "skin" layer formed on the surface of a substrate, a porous bulk layer formed on the first porous "skin" layer, and a second porous "skin" layer formed on the surface of the porous bulk layer. This abstract is intended as a scanning tool for purposes of searching in the particular art and is not intended to be limiting of the present disclosure.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A multi-layer block copolymer material, the multi-layer block copolymer material comprising a self-assembled block copolymer; wherein the self-assembled block copolymer comprises: a first skin layer; a second skin layer; and a bulk layer disposed between the first skin layer and the second skin layer; and wherein each of the first skin layer, the second skin layer and the bulk layer comprise pores. 2. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 1 , wherein the self-assembled block copolymer further comprises: a first interfacial layer disposed between the first skin layer; and/or a second interfacial layer disposed between the second skin layer and the bulk layer. 3. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 1 , further comprising a substrate disposed on a side of the first skin layer opposite the bulk layer. 4. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 3, wherein the substrate has pores; and wherein the pores have an average pore diameter ranging from about 0.1 pm to about 10 pm. 5. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 3, wherein the substrate has pores; and wherein the pores have an average pore diameter ranging from about 0.1 pm to about 3 pm. 6. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 3, wherein the substrate has pores; and wherein the pores have an average pore diameter ranging from 0.1 pm to about 1 pm. 7. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 3, wherein the substrate has pores; and wherein the pores have an average pore diameter ranging from about 0.22 pm to about 1 pm. 

The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 3, wherein the substrate has pores; and wherein the pores have an average pore diameter ranging from about 0.45 pm to about 1 pm. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 1, wherein the bulk layer comprises macropores. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 9, wherein the bulk layer further comprises super-macropores. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 9, wherein the bulk layer further comprises mesopores. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 1, wherein the first skin layer comprises micropores, mesopores, or combination thereof. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 12, wherein the first skin layer comprises pores having a pore diameter of from about 5 nm to about 100 nm. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 12, wherein the first skin layer comprises pores having a pore diameter of from about 10 nm to about 50 nm. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 12, wherein the first skin layer comprises pores having a pore diameter of from about 15 nm to about 25 nm. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 12, wherein the first skin layer comprises pores having a pore diameter of from about 20 nm to about 40 nm. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 12, wherein the first skin layer comprises pores having a pore diameter of from about 30 nm to about 40 nm. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 1, wherein the first skin layer is isoporous. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 1, wherein the second skin layer comprises micropores, mesopores, or combination thereof. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 19, wherein the second skin layer comprises pores having a pore diameter of from about 5 nm to about 50nm. comprises pores having a pore diameter of from about 5 nm to about 10 nm. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 19, wherein the second skin layer comprises pores having a pore diameter of from about 20 nm to about 25 nm . The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim: 19, wherein the second skin layer comprises pores having a pore diameter of from about 25 nm to about 30 nm. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 19, wherein the second skin layer comprises pores having a pore diameter of from about 30 nm to about 50 nm. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 1 , wherein the second skin layer is isoporous. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 1, wherein the second skin layer having average pore diameters less than an average pore diameter of the first skin layer. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 29, wherein the average pore diameter of the second skin layer is from about 5% to about 200% less than average pore diameter of the first skin layer. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 29, wherein the average pore diameter of the second skin layer is at least about 5% less than average pore diameter of the first skin layer. first skin layer. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 1, wherein the second skin layer having average pore diameters less than an average pore diameter of phase inverted pores of the bulk layer. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 39, wherein the average pore diameter of the second skin layer is at least about 750% less than the average pore diameter of the phase inverted pores of the bulk layer. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 39, wherein the average pore diameter of the second skin layer is at least about 1000% less than the average pore diameter of the phase inverted pores of the bulk layer. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 1 , further comprising nanoparticles disposed within the pores of the first skin layer, the second skin layer, or a combination thereof. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 47, wherein the nanoparticles comprise a metal, an alloy, a metal oxide, or any combination thereof. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 47, wherein the nanoparticles comprise core, core-shell or core-multishell semiconductor nanoparticles. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 47, wherein the nanoparticles comprise inorganic phosphors. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 1 , further comprising a homopolymer. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 1 , further comprising a small molecule. The multi-layer block copolymer material of claim 1, wherein the material is a film or membrane. A filtration device comprising a multi-layer block copolymer material according to claim 1. The filtration device of claim 54, wherein the filtration device comprises an inlet for 

providing a sample to the filtration device; and an outlet from which to collect a filtrate material. The filtration device of claim 54, wherein the device is a protein purification device. The filtration device of claim 54, wherein the device is a virus reduction or removal device. The filtration device of claim 54, wherein the device is a feedstream purification device. The filtration device of claim 54, wherein the device is a water purification device. The filtration device of claim 54, wherein the device is a solute separation device. A method of filtration, wherein the method comprises providing a sample to the filtration device of claim 54 via inlet to the filtration device; and collecting a filtrate material that has pass through the filtration device. The method of claim 61 , wherein the sample comprises a plurality of proteins. The method of claim 62, wherein the plurality of proteins comprises at least one antibody. The method of claim 62, wherein the plurality of proteins comprises at least one recombinant protein. The method of claim 61 , wherein the sample comprises a plurality of viruses. The method of claim 61 , wherein the sample comprises a solution. The method of claim 66, wherein the solution is an aqueous solution. The method of claim 66, wherein the solution is a non-aqueous solution. The method of claim 68, wherein the non-aqueous solution comprises at least one polar solvent, at least one non-polar solvent, or combinations thereof. The method of claim 61 , wherein the sample comprises a feedstream. The method of claim 70, wherein the feedstream is a feedstream from a bioreactor. The method of claim 70, wherein the feedstream is a feedstream from a sewage system. A method to prepare the multi-layer block copolymer material, the method comprising the steps of: (a) providing a deposition solution comprising at least one multiblock copolymer and a solvent system; 

(b) depositing the deposition solution on a substrate to form an initial film; (c) removing a portion of a solvent of the solvent system from the initial film to form a partial solvent removed initial film; and (d) contacting the partial solvent removed initial film with a phase separation solvent system, thereby forming the multi-layer block copolymer material; wherein the substrate has pores; and wherein the pores have an average pore diameter ranging from about 0.1 pm to about 10 pm. The method of claim 73, wherein the solvent system comprises at least dioxane. The method of claim 74, wherein the solvent system comprises about 70 wt% dioxane. The method of claim 74, wherein the solvent system further comprises tetrahydrofuran, acetone, or a combination thereof. The method of claim 76, wherein the tetrahydrofuran, acetone, or a combination thereof is present in an amount of about 30 wt%. The method of claim 73, wherein the substrate has pores; and wherein the pores have an average pore diameter ranging from about 0.1 pm to about 5 pm, The method of claim 78, wherein the pores have an average pore diameter ranging from 0,1 pm to about 3 pm. The method of claim 78, wherein the pores have an average pore diameter ranging from 0,22 pm to about 1 pm. The method of claim 73, wherein the phase separation solvent system comprises water. The method of claim 73, wherein the removing a portion of the solvent of the solvent system from the initial film to form a partial solvent removed initial film comprises removing from about 1 wt% to about 80 wt% of at least one solvent from the solvent system. The method of claim 73, wherein the contacting the partial solvent removed initial film with a phase separation solvent system comprises a water coagulation bath. 84. The method of claim 73, further comprising removing the substrate. 85. A multi-layer block copolymer material made by the method of claim 73.
